code: op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(1, 1, 3, 1)) cograph::splot(fit, layout = "circle", title = "circle") cograph::splot(fit, layout = "spring", seed = 11, title = "spring") par(op)

title: Visualizing networks with cograph
text: node arguments set fill, border, label size, shape. scale_nodes_by argument sizes node centrality measure node_size_range sets smallest largest radius, node area reads structural importance. Sizing nodes strength makes connected construct largest page.
code: cograph::splot(   fit,   layout = "circle",   scale_nodes_by = "strength",   node_size_range = c(4, 12),   node_fill = c("#4C78A8", "#F58518", "#54A24B", "#B279A2", "#E45756"),   node_border_color = "white",   node_border_width = 2,   label_size = 0.9,   title = "Node size scaled by strength" )

title: Visualizing networks with cograph
text: signed psychometric network, edge encoding carries substantive result. edge_positive_color edge_negative_color arguments set two sign colours edge_width_range sets mapping magnitude width. threshold argument hides edges absolute weight, thins dense graph strongest associations, edge_labels prints weight retained edge.  cograph::plot_edge_weights() draws distribution edge weights , reads spread associations location strong edges without graph layout.
code: cograph::splot(   fit,   layout = "circle",   threshold = 0.05,   edge_width_range = c(0.5, 5),   edge_positive_color = "#2A9D8F",   edge_negative_color = "#E76F51",   edge_labels = TRUE,   edge_label_size = 0.7,   edge_label_bg = "white",   title = "Thresholded edge weights" ) cograph::plot_edge_weights(fit)

title: Visualizing networks with cograph
text: bootstrap diagnostics computed psychnets drawn base-R plot methods, covered fully companion vignette. net_boot() resamples data refits network resample, plot() result defaults edge-weight confidence intervals.  bootstrap object holds retained draws pairwise edge-difference test. type = "edge_diff", plot() draws significance-box matrix edges differ one another.  difference_test() returns pairwise differences tidy table, plot() style = "forest" draws difference point confidence interval effect sizes matter box display.
code: set.seed(1) bs <- net_boot(SRL_GPT, method = "glasso", n_boot = 250, cores = 1) plot(bs) plot(bs, type = "edge_diff") plot(difference_test(bs, type = "edge"), style = "forest")

title: Clean-Room Base-R Psychometric Network Estimation
text: Clean-room, self-certifying estimation cross-sectional psychometric network models base R. — Mohammed Saqr psychnets implements principal cross-sectional estimators used psychometric network analysis entirely base R. package provides correlation partial-correlation networks, EBIC-regularized Gaussian graphical model (graphical lasso), together nonparanormal unregularized stepwise variants, information-filtering networks including Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph (TMFG) Local–Global inverse covariance estimation, relative-importance networks, Ising model binary variables, mixed graphical model. Whereas estimators conventionally accessed high-level R packages delegate numerical optimization compiled Fortran C/C++ libraries, psychnets reimplements complete estimation procedures directly R. particular, graphical lasso estimation commonly performed qgraph bootnet, invoke Fortran implementation provided glasso, Ising mixed graphical models typically estimated IsingFit mgm, rely compiled optimization routines glmnet. psychnets invokes none packages underlying compiled numerical libraries. package dependencies standard R distributions stats, parallel, graphics, grDevices. every regularized estimator, psychnets reports numerical certificate optimality form Karush–Kuhn–Tucker (KKT) residual associated convex optimization problem defining estimator. KKT residual quantifies degree necessary first-order optimality conditions satisfied therefore provides direct measure convergence. Residual values approaching zero indicate numerical solution effectively stationary respect objective function. diagnostic computed directly fitted solution, evaluation independent external optimization software require comparison reference implementations. implementation philosophy psychnets based four complementary design principles. First, estimation procedure fully transparent, statistical methodology numerical optimization algorithms implemented explicitly R, allowing every computational step inspected reproduced without reference opaque compiled libraries. Second, estimation reproducible convergence behaviour determined user-accessible numerical criteria rather implementation-specific tolerances embedded within precompiled binaries. Third, package maintains minimal dependency structure avoiding external numerical libraries glasso, glmnet, qgraph, Matrix, together associated compiled dependency chains, including Rcpp RcppArmadillo. Finally, estimation directly auditable every regularized solution accompanied optimality certificate quantifies numerical accuracy respect objective function minimized. Consequently, correctness estimated network can assessed directly fitted model rather inferred agreement independent software implementation. optimization problem solved numerical stationarity model selection performed using standard formulation Extended Bayesian Information Criterion (EBIC), network recovery reflects theoretical properties estimator rather artefacts incomplete optimization. consequence, edge selection influenced premature termination numerical algorithm, thereby avoiding inclusion edges may arise solely insufficient convergence underlying optimization procedure. Validation performed using simulated data known generating models published psychometric datasets verify correctness every implemented estimator. Across methods, psychnets reproduces established reference implementations numerical precision attainable packages, remaining differences attributable floating-point arithmetic solver convergence tolerances. EBIC graphical lasso, practical limit determined convergence criterion underlying glasso implementation used qgraph, approximately (10^{-4}). psychnets instead solves optimization problem substantially tighter tolerances reports Karush–Kuhn–Tucker residual every regularized fit, providing independent numerical certificate returned solution satisfies optimality conditions estimator.

code: casedrop_reliability(   data,   method = "glasso",   drop_prop = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1),   iter = 100L,   threshold = 0.7,   certainty = 0.95,   cor_method = c("spearman", "pearson", "kendall"),   labels = NULL,   ... )

title: Edge-weight stability coefficient (case-dropping subset bootstrap) — casedrop_reliability
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations), psychnet_group (case-dropped per level). method Estimator (see psychnet()). Default "glasso". drop_prop Proportions cases drop. Default seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1). iter Subsets per proportion. Default 100. threshold Minimum acceptable edge-vector correlation. Default 0.7. certainty Probability correlation must exceed threshold. code: set.seed(1) x1 <- stats::rnorm(400); x2 <- stats::rnorm(400) mod <- rep(0:1, each = 200) y <- x1 * (mod == 1) + stats::rnorm(400)   # x1-y edge only when mod == 1 d <- data.frame(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, y = y, mod = mod) # `moderators=` is the one estimator that needs glmnet (a Suggested package). if (requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit <- mgm_fit(d, types = c("g", "g", "g", "c"), moderators = 4)   condition(fit, value = 1) } #> <psychnet> mgm_moderated network #>   nodes: 4   edges: 1   (undirected)

text: Selects L1 penalty extended BIC (Foygel & Drton 2010) log-spaced path, refits chosen penalty machine precision returned network certified global optimum convex objective. Equivalent purpose qgraph::EBICglasso() / bootnet's "EBICglasso" default, pure base R self-certified (see glasso_kkt()).

title: EBIC-regularized Gaussian graphical model (graphical lasso) — ebic_glasso
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations). Optional cor_matrix supplied. cor_matrix Optional correlation matrix; given, n required data ignored. n Sample size (required cor_matrix supplied). gamma EBIC hyperparameter. Default 0.5. nlambda Number penalties path. Default 100. lambda_min_ratio Smallest penalty fraction largest. Default 0.01. threshold Partial correlations absolute value set zero. Default 0. cor_method Correlation used data supplied: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "kendall", "auto" (polychoric/polyserial ordinal items, qgraph::cor_auto / bootnet default). See cor_auto(). na_method Missing-data handling data supplied: "pairwise" (default, pairwise-complete correlations + nearest-PD projection) "listwise" (drop incomplete rows). Identical complete data. native Solver switch. TRUE (default) uses psychnet's pure-R, dependency-free, self-certified solver. FALSE delegates fixed-penalty solve established glasso Fortran package (Suggests) speed byte-identical glasso/qgraph output, looser convergence (reported $kkt shows glasso's tolerance rather ~1e-11). labels Optional node labels.

title: Action frequencies from an event log — event_frequencies
text: Converts long event log – one row per event, actor, action, optionally session time – frequency table: one row per occasion, one column per distinct action, holding action's count. conversion mirrors "frequency" format Nestimate event pipeline. occasion one (actor, session) cell; compute_sessions = TRUE time column used split actor sessions wherever gap consecutive events exceeds time_threshold. frequency input psychnet() builds internally event data (source = "eventdata").

title: Action frequencies from an event log — event_frequencies
code: event_frequencies(   data,   actor = "Actor",   action = "Action",   session = NULL,   time = NULL,   compute_sessions = TRUE,   time_threshold = 900 )

title: Action frequencies from an event log — event_frequencies
text: data long event log (data frame), one row per event. actor Column(s) naming actor / subject. Default "Actor". action Column naming action / state. Default "Action". session Optional column(s) naming explicit session within actor. time Optional column used compute sessions gaps (see compute_sessions); used temporal model. compute_sessions TRUE, split actor sessions time gaps (new session starts gap exceeds time_threshold). Default TRUE. time_threshold Maximum gap (units time, seconds timestamp) consecutive events new session begins. Default 900 (15 minutes), Nestimate.

title: Action frequencies from an event log — event_frequencies
text: data.frame actor column, session index, one integer count column per action (one row per occasion).

title: Action frequencies from an event log — event_frequencies
code: ev <- data.frame(   Actor   = rep(c("a", "b"), each = 6),   Session = rep(rep(1:2, each = 3), 2),   Action  = c("read","quiz","read", "quiz","read","note",               "note","note","read", "read","quiz","quiz")) event_frequencies(ev, session = "Session") #>   actor session note quiz read #> 1     a     a.1    0    1    2 #> 2     a     a.2    1    1    1 #> 3     b     b.1    2    0    1 #> 4     b     b.2    0    2    1

what: Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect) — ggm_modselect
title: Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect) — ggm_modselect
text: Selects GGM extended-BIC model search edge sets generated glasso path, refitting unregularized maximum-likelihood precision candidate graph, optional stepwise add/drop search. Unlike graphical lasso, retained edges shrunk. Equivalent purpose qgraph::ggmModSelect(), pure base R self-certified via ggm_support_kkt().

title: Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect) — ggm_modselect
code: ggm_modselect(   data = NULL,   cor_matrix = NULL,   n = NULL,   gamma = 0,   stepwise = TRUE,   nlambda = 100L,   lambda_min_ratio = 0.01,   threshold = 0,   cor_method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall", "auto"),   na_method = c("pairwise", "listwise"),   native = TRUE,   labels = NULL )

title: Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect) — ggm_modselect
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations). Optional cor_matrix supplied. cor_matrix Optional correlation matrix; given, n required. n Sample size (required cor_matrix supplied). gamma EBIC hyperparameter. Default 0, matching qgraph::ggmModSelect(): selected graph refit unregularized MLE (edge shrinkage), extra EBIC penalty needed, gamma 0 (plain BIC) method's intended setting. (regularized GGMs ebic_glasso() huge_network() keep gamma 0.5.) stepwise TRUE (default), refine best glasso-path graph greedy single-edge add/drop search. nlambda Number glasso penalties scanned candidate graphs. lambda_min_ratio Smallest penalty fraction largest. threshold Partial correlations absolute value zeroed. Default 0. cor_method Correlation used data supplied: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "kendall", "auto" (polychoric/polyserial, qgraph/bootnet default ordinal items). See cor_auto(). na_method Missing-data handling data supplied: "pairwise" (default) "listwise". See ebic_glasso(). native Solver switch generating candidate supports: TRUE (default) uses pure-R solver; FALSE delegates glasso Fortran package (Suggests). reported precision unregularized refit either way. See ebic_glasso(). labels Optional node labels.

title: Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect) — ggm_modselect
text: psychnet object whose $weights partial-correlation matrix, $precision, $support (selected graph), $gamma, $ebic, $cor_matrix, $kkt.

title: Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect) — ggm_modselect
code: S <- 0.5^abs(outer(1:6, 1:6, "-")) ggm_modselect(cor_matrix = S, n = 250) #> <psychnet> ggm network #>   nodes: 6   edges: 5   (undirected) #>   optimality (KKT residual): 2.22e-16

what: Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity residual — ggm_support_kkt
title: Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity residual — ggm_support_kkt
text: Certificate unregularized Gaussian graphical model whose precision constrained fixed graph (estimator behind ggm_modselect() logo_network()). maximum-likelihood / maximum-entropy conditions Gaussian Markov random field graph \(G\) exact: \(W_{ij} = S_{ij}\) every \((,j)\) graph diagonal (\(W = \Theta^{-1}\)), \(\Theta_{ij} = 0\) every \((,j)\) graph. near-zero return certifies constrained optimum reference solver.

title: Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity residual — ggm_support_kkt
code: ggm_support_kkt(theta, cor_matrix, support, active_tol = 1e-08)

title: Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity residual — ggm_support_kkt
text: theta Precision matrix test. cor_matrix Correlation / covariance model fit . support Logical p x p matrix; TRUE edge allowed. active_tol Magnitude -support entry counts nonzero violation.

title: Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity residual — ggm_support_kkt
text: Maximum absolute stationarity violation (scalar); 0 = exact optimum.

title: Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity residual — ggm_support_kkt
code: S <- 0.4^abs(outer(1:6, 1:6, "-")) fit <- ggm_modselect(cor_matrix = S, n = 250) ggm_support_kkt(fit$precision, S, fit$support) #> [1] 2.220446e-16

title: Graphical-lasso stationarity (KKT) residual — glasso_kkt
text: dependency-free correctness certificate fitted Gaussian graphical model. convex objective $$\min_{\Theta \succ 0} -\log\det\Theta + \mathrm{tr}(S\Theta) +       \rho \sum_{\neq j} |\Theta_{ij}|$$ (-diagonal penalty), let \(W = \Theta^{-1}\). subgradient optimality conditions \(W_{ii} = S_{ii}\); \(W_{ij} - S_{ij} = \rho\,\mathrm{sign}(\Theta_{ij})\) \(\Theta_{ij} \neq 0\); \(|W_{ij} - S_{ij}| \le \rho\) otherwise. strict convexity, precision matrix zero violation unique global optimum, near-zero return certifies correctness independently reference solver.

title: Graphical-lasso stationarity (KKT) residual — glasso_kkt
code: glasso_kkt(theta, cor_matrix, rho, active_tol = 1e-08)

title: Graphical-lasso stationarity (KKT) residual — glasso_kkt
text: theta Precision matrix test. cor_matrix Correlation / covariance model fit . rho Scalar penalty. active_tol Magnitude -diagonal entry "active".

title: Graphical-lasso stationarity (KKT) residual — glasso_kkt
text: Maximum absolute stationarity violation (scalar); 0 = exact optimum.

title: Graphical-lasso stationarity (KKT) residual — glasso_kkt
code: S <- 0.5^abs(outer(1:5, 1:5, "-")) fit <- ebic_glasso(cor_matrix = S, n = 200) glasso_kkt(fit$precision, S, fit$lambda) #> [1] 7.168638e-11

what: Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit — glm_lasso_kkt
title: Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit — glm_lasso_kkt
text: Dependency-free correctness certificate nodewise lasso, analogous glasso_kkt() graphical lasso. standardized predictors X fitted mean mu (identity link gaussian, logistic binomial), subgradient conditions \(n^{-1} X_j^\top (y - \mu) = \lambda\, \mathrm{sign}(\beta_j)\) active coordinates \(|n^{-1} X_j^\top (y - \mu)| \le \lambda\) otherwise. Near-zero certifies penalized-likelihood optimum.

title: Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit — glm_lasso_kkt
code: glm_lasso_kkt(   X,   y,   b0,   beta,   lambda,   family = "gaussian",   weights = NULL,   active_tol = 1e-08 )

title: Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit — glm_lasso_kkt
text: X Standardized predictor matrix (mean 0, unit variance columns). y Response. b0 Fitted intercept. beta Fitted (standardized) coefficients. lambda Penalty. family "gaussian" "binomial". weights Optional observation weights (NULL = unweighted). active_tol Magnitude coefficient "active".

title: Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit — glm_lasso_kkt
text: Maximum absolute stationarity violation (scalar). Near-zero certifies fit penalized-likelihood optimum.

title: Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit — glm_lasso_kkt
code: set.seed(1) x <- scale(matrix(stats::rnorm(200 * 3), 200, 3)) y <- as.numeric(x %*% c(0.5, 0, -0.3) + stats::rnorm(200)) fit <- stats::lm.fit(cbind(1, x), y) glm_lasso_kkt(x, y, fit$coefficients[1], fit$coefficients[-1], lambda = 0) #> [1] 7.549517e-17

what: Ising network for binary data — ising_fit
title: Ising network for binary data — ising_fit
text: Estimates Ising model nodewise L1-penalized logistic regression EBIC selection, combined (default) rule. Equivalent purpose IsingFit::IsingFit(), pure base R self-certified: node's regression reports stationarity (KKT) residual (see glm_lasso_kkt()).

title: Ising network for binary data — ising_fit

title: Ising network for binary data — ising_fit
text: data Binary (0/1) data frame matrix (rows = observations). gamma EBIC hyperparameter. Default 0.25. rule Edge-combination rule: "" (default) "". nlambda Number penalties per nodewise path. Default 100. lambda_min_ratio Smallest penalty fraction largest. min_sum Minimum row sum-score (number endorsed items); rows dropped fitting. NULL (default) keeps every row. weights Optional non-negative observation weights, one per retained row. NULL (default) unweighted. na_method Missing-data handling: "pairwise" (default) single-imputes column observed values (mode binary), keeping full sample; "listwise" drops incomplete rows. Identical complete data. native Solver switch. TRUE (default) uses psychnet's pure-R, dependency-free, self-certified L1 logistic path (KKT ~1e-9). FALSE delegates per-node fit glmnet package IsingFit EBIC path, returned $weights/$thresholds byte-match IsingFit::IsingFit() (~1e-16) cost glmnet's looser self-certificate. native = FALSE needs optional glmnet package (Suggests); weights/min_sum supported native = TRUE . labels Optional node labels.

title: Mixed graphical model — mgm_fit
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations); columns continuous binary (0/1). gamma EBIC hyperparameter. Default 0.25. types Optional character vector node types ("g" gaussian, "c" binary); auto-detected NULL. nlambda Number penalties per nodewise path. Default 100. lambda_min_ratio Smallest penalty fraction largest. threshold Post-selection coefficient threshold: "LW" (default), "HW", "none", matching mgm::mgm(). rule Edge-combination rule: "" (default) "". moderators Optional single column index moderator variable. supplied, fits moderated MGM (variable moderates every pairwise edge) returns psychnet_moderated object read condition(); glmnet-based, weights supported mode. weights Optional non-negative observation weights, one per row (NA-prepared) data. NULL (default) unweighted. na_method Missing-data handling: "pairwise" (default) single-imputes column observed values (mean continuous, mode binary), keeping full sample; "listwise" drops incomplete rows. native Solver switch. TRUE (default) uses psychnet's pure-R, dependency-free, self-certified L1 path (KKT ~1e-9). FALSE delegates per-node fit glmnet package mgm's exact EBIC/LW path (gaussian lasso continuous nodes, 2-class multinomial lasso binary nodes), returned edge magnitudes byte-match abs(mgm::mgm()$pairwise$wadj) (~1e-6) cost glmnet's looser self-certificate. native = FALSE needs optional glmnet package (Suggests); weights supported native = TRUE . labels Optional node labels.

title: Mixed graphical model — mgm_fit
text: psychnet object whose $weights symmetric standardized weight matrix, $types $kkt (worst nodewise residual). binary-binary edge carries sign nodewise-logistic coefficient; mgm::mgm() reports edge magnitude (sign undefined categorical-categorical interaction), compare edges abs(). Continuous columns standardized internally, binary predictors enter graph 0/1 dummy scale, binary-response logit coefficients converted mgm's two-class multinomial scale edge aggregation. conventions edge magnitudes match mgm::mgm closely gaussian-gaussian, gaussian-binary, binary-binary edges alike; weak edges near EBIC/threshold boundary can still differ support penalty selected independent base-R path.

title: Mixed graphical model — mgm_fit
code: set.seed(1) f <- stats::rnorm(400) g1 <- f + stats::rnorm(400); g2 <- f + stats::rnorm(400) b1 <- (f + stats::rnorm(400) > 0) * 1L d <- data.frame(g1 = g1, g2 = g2, b1 = b1, n = stats::rnorm(400)) mgm_fit(d) #> <psychnet> mgm network #>   nodes: 4   edges: 3   (undirected) #>   optimality (KKT residual): 6.84e-09

title: Bootstrap a psychometric network — net_boot
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations). method Estimator (see psychnet()). Default "glasso". n_boot Number bootstrap resamples. Default 1000. ci Confidence level percentile intervals. Default 0.95. measures Centrality measures bootstrap. Defaults two recommended psychometric networks ("strength", "expected_influence"); "betweenness"/"closeness" custom measures (via centrality_fn) also accepted. See net_centralities(). centrality_fn Optional function supplying non-built-measures (see net_centralities()). predictability Logical; TRUE estimator returns precision matrix (GGM family), bootstrap node predictability (R^2) report interval. Default FALSE. threshold Logical; TRUE, also return observed network every edge whose bootstrap interval includes zero set zero ($thresholded). Default FALSE. diff_test Logical; TRUE, also return two-sided bootstrap difference p-value matrices edges ($edge_diff_p, NULL past 500 edges) centrality measure ($centrality_diff_p). Default FALSE. p_adjust Multiple-comparison adjustment applied difference p-value matrices (stats::p.adjust method). Default "none". labels Optional node labels. cores Number CPU cores resample loop. NULL (default) uses two thirds detected cores; 1 forces serial run. Parallelism uses forking (parallel::mclapply) falls back serial Windows. every resample index drawn parent process fitting, result identical number cores reproducible set.seed(). engine Optional estimator engine forwarded resample fit (e.g. "base"/"glasso" glasso, "base"/"glmnet" ising/mgm). NULL (default) uses estimator's default. ... Passed estimator.

title: Network Comparison Test — net_compare
text: Permutation test whether two groups' Gaussian graphical models differ, three invariants: global strength (M), maximum edge difference (S), per-edge differences (E). Networks EBIC graphical lassos (clean-room pure R). Equivalent purpose NetworkComparisonTest::NCT().

title: Network Comparison Test — net_compare

title: Network Comparison Test — net_compare
text: data1, data2 Numeric data frames/matrices columns. iter Number permutations. Default 1000. gamma EBIC hyperparameter. Default 0.5. paired Logical; within-row swapping paired designs. Default FALSE. abs Logical; compare absolute edge weights. Default TRUE. weighted Logical; FALSE, binarize networks first. Default TRUE. p_adjust Multiple-comparison adjustment per-edge p-values (stats::p.adjust method). Default "none".

title: Network Comparison Test — net_compare
text: object class psychnet_nct $nw1, $nw2, $M, $S, $E (observed, perm, p_value); $E also carries edge_names, /data frame aligned per-edge vector.

title: Network Comparison Test — net_compare
code: set.seed(1) a <- matrix(stats::rnorm(150 * 5), 150, 5) b <- matrix(stats::rnorm(150 * 5), 150, 5) colnames(a) <- colnames(b) <- paste0("V", 1:5) fit <- net_compare(a, b, iter = 25) fit #> Network Comparison Test (25 permutations) #>   Global strength (M): observed 0.000, p = 1.000 #>   Network structure (S): observed 0.000, p = 1.000

title: Estimate a psychometric network — psychnet
text: package's main entry point. Supply data method; everything else optional fine-grained control. psychnet() reads two kinds input picks right one automatically (source = "auto"):

title: Estimate a psychometric network — psychnet
code: psychnet(   data,   method = c("glasso", "cor", "pcor", "ising", "mgm", "huge", "ggm", "tmfg", "logo",     "relimp", "ising_sampler"),   threshold = 0,   gamma = NULL,   labels = NULL,   vars = NULL,   group = NULL,   source = c("auto", "data", "eventdata"),   actor = NULL,   action = "Action",   session = NULL,   time = NULL,   compute_sessions = TRUE,   time_threshold = 900,   id = NULL,   standardize = TRUE,   ... )

title: Estimate a psychometric network — psychnet
text: data numeric data frame / matrix (rows = observations), long event log actor supplied. method Estimator. One "glasso" (default), "ggm", "tmfg", "logo", "relimp", "ising", "ising_sampler", "huge", "mgm", "cor", "pcor". qgraph/bootnet names accepted aliases (e.g. "EBICglasso" -> "glasso", "ggmModSelect" -> "ggm"). Event data restricted Gaussian graphical methods. threshold Absolute-weight threshold edges zeroed (forwarded methods take : cor, pcor, glasso, huge, ggm, logo). gamma EBIC hyperparameter. NULL (default) keeps method's default (0.5 regularized Gaussian graphical models, 0 ggm, 0.25 ising/mgm); set override. Forwarded regularized methods. labels Optional node labels. vars variables build network . Defaults every variable. Selected tidy way: name range (motivation:regulation), column-index range (3:9), vector names (c(joy, fear) c("joy", "fear")), single name – grammar subset()'s select=. (event log, actions become variables, vars selects feature columns data already numeric table.) group Optional grouping column(s). supplied, one network estimated per level group psychnet_group (named list networks) returned, plots grid iterated per level framework verbs. source Input kind: "auto" (default; event log actor given, otherwise numeric table), "data", "eventdata". actor, action, session, time Event-log columns. actor (, default, action = "Action") name subject event; session explicit within-actor grouping, time used compute sessions gaps. Supplying actor selects source = "eventdata". compute_sessions, time_threshold Split actor sessions time gaps (new session starts gap exceeds time_threshold, default 900 s). compute_sessions defaults TRUE; -op time supplied. id Actor column event log already reduced numeric feature table (one row per occasion) rather raw events. standardize nested event data (several occasions per actor), TRUE (default) removes actor clustering person-centering fits single network; FALSE returns within/pair instead. ... Passed underlying estimator (e.g. cor_method= correlation-based methods, npn= "huge", rule= Ising methods, alpha= correlation / "ising_sampler" methods).

title: Estimate a psychometric network — psychnet
text: psychnet object; psychnet_group (named list networks) group supplied; , nested event data standardize = FALSE, psychnet_multilevel object carrying $within $networks.

title: Estimate a psychometric network — psychnet
text: numeric table (source = "data") – ordinary cross-sectional case: one row per observation, one column per variable; long event log (source = "eventdata") – one row per event, actor action column (optionally session / time). log converted action frequencies event_frequencies() , actors contribute several occasions, decomposed within- -actor networks. Passing actor switches automatically. method speaks package's short vocabulary – "glasso", "ggm", "tmfg", "logo", "relimp", "ising", "ising_sampler", "huge", "mgm", "cor", "pcor". interoperability also accepts qgraph/bootnet spellings ("EBICglasso", "ggmModSelect", "TMFG", "LoGo", "IsingFit", "IsingSampler"), resolve estimators. Whichever pass , stored $method short name.

title: Estimate a psychometric network — psychnet
code: x <- matrix(stats::rnorm(200 * 5), 200, 5) colnames(x) <- c("joy", "fear", "calm", "anger", "trust") psychnet(x, method = "glasso") #> <psychnet> glasso network #>   nodes: 5   edges: 0   (undirected) #>   lambda: 0.1506   gamma: 0.5 #>   optimality (KKT residual): 0.00e+00 psychnet(x, method = "pcor", vars = joy:anger)     # name range #> <psychnet> pcor network #>   nodes: 4   edges: 6   (undirected) psychnet(x, method = "glasso", vars = 1:3)         # index range #> <psychnet> glasso network #>   nodes: 3   edges: 0   (undirected) #>   lambda: 0.06534   gamma: 0.5 #>   optimality (KKT residual): 0.00e+00 psychnet(x, method = "EBICglasso")                 # qgraph alias, same result #> <psychnet> glasso network #>   nodes: 5   edges: 0   (undirected) #>   lambda: 0.1506   gamma: 0.5 #>   optimality (KKT residual): 0.00e+00  ev <- data.frame(   Actor  = rep(paste0("s", 1:30), each = 20),   Action = sample(c("read", "quiz", "note", "watch"), 600, replace = TRUE)) psychnet(ev, actor = "Actor", action = "Action")   # event data, auto-detected #> <psychnet> glasso network #>   nodes: 4   edges: 6   (undirected) #>   lambda: 0.004916   gamma: 0.5 #>   optimality (KKT residual): 6.61e-10  d <- data.frame(g = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 100),                 matrix(stats::rnorm(200 * 4), 200, 4,                        dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("V", 1:4)))) psychnet(d, method = "glasso", group = "g")         # one network per level #> <psychnet_group> 2 networks by g (method: glasso) #>  group nodes edges   n #>      A     4     0 100 #>      B     4     0 100

title: Relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley) — relimp_network
text: Builds directed network edge predictor -> outcome predictor's LMG (Shapley) share outcome node's regression R-squared. Equivalent purpose relaimpo::calc.relimp(type = "lmg") applied nodewise / bootnet's "relimp" default, pure base R self-certified via lmg_certificate().

title: Relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley) — relimp_network
code: relimp_network(   data = NULL,   cor_matrix = NULL,   cor_method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall", "auto"),   max_nodes = 21L,   normalized = FALSE,   na_method = c("pairwise", "listwise"),   labels = NULL )

title: Relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley) — relimp_network
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations). Optional cor_matrix supplied. cor_matrix Optional correlation matrix. cor_method Correlation data supplied: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "kendall", "auto" (polychoric/polyserial; see cor_auto()). max_nodes Refuse run many nodes (cost grows 2^(p-1) per node). Default 21. normalized TRUE, rescale outcome's incoming importance shares sum 1, matching bootnet/relaimpo's normalized reporting. default FALSE keeps raw LMG shares sum outcome R-squared. na_method Missing-data handling data supplied: "pairwise" (default) "listwise". See ebic_glasso(). labels Optional node labels.

title: Relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley) — relimp_network
text: psychnet object whose $weights directed importance matrix (weights[k, j] = importance k outcome j), $r2 (per-node full-model R-squared), $cor_matrix, $kkt (decomposition residual). normalized = FALSE (default) outcome's incoming shares sum R-squared (colSums($weights) == $r2); normalized = TRUE rescaled sum 1, $raw_importance holds unscaled shares, $kkt (like lmg_certificate()) computed raw shares.

title: Relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley) — relimp_network
code: S <- 0.4^abs(outer(1:5, 1:5, "-")) relimp_network(cor_matrix = S) #> <psychnet> relimp network #>   nodes: 5   edges: 20   (directed) #>   optimality (KKT residual): 5.55e-17

title: Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph (TMFG) — tmfg_network
text: Builds sparse, planar, chordal association network greedily retaining 3(p - 2) informative edges (Massara et al. 2016). Equivalent purpose NetworkToolbox::TMFG() / bootnet's "TMFG" default, pure base R; correctness certified structurally tmfg_certificate().

title: Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph (TMFG) — tmfg_network
text: data Numeric data frame matrix (rows = observations). Optional cor_matrix supplied. cor_matrix Optional correlation matrix. n Accepted ignored. TMFG structural filter needs sample size; argument exists uniform (cor_matrix=, n=) call shared estimators partial-match na_method. cor_method Correlation data supplied: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "kendall", "auto" (polychoric/polyserial; see cor_auto()). na_method Missing-data handling data supplied: "pairwise" (default) "listwise". See ebic_glasso(). labels Optional node labels.
